macro_summary: Monday's session was dominated by the re-escalation of US-Iran tensions as President Trump reinstated a blockade on Iranian ships and proposed a 20% transit fee on all Strait of Hormuz cargo. Crude surged 7.1% to $79.56, the biggest jump since April, while the Nasdaq fell 2.0% and semis plunged 5.2%. Oil-driven inflation fears rekindled a 46.5% probability of a July Fed rate hike, according to CME FedWatch, ahead of today's critical CPI report.
commodities_note: WTI spiked to $79.56 on the renewed blockade; the Strait of Hormuz passes roughly 20% of global oil, so supply-premium risks are acute. Gold slipped 1.2% to $4,018 as firm yields offset safe-haven bids. The dollar index was flat.

The NIM compression twin headwinds (US10Y >4.20% and MAS S$NEER) are unrelenting. No entry. The cluster divergence is nil — all are extended and awaiting NIM relief, which likely requires a US10Y break below 4.20% or an MAS pivot in October.

singapore.key_development: No change; US10Y held at 4.61% and MAS appreciation persists. All three remain non-actionable.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster got crushed (-5.2% on SOXX) as the Hormuz blockade re-escalation triggered a broad risk-off move. NVDA is the standout: it has pulled back to a BUY-zone while most peers are still extended or deeply oversold without a clear floor. MU is approaching a WATCH-level setup but needs volume. INTC and AMD are stuck in CAUTION due to valuation and execution risks. The cluster’s immediate fate hinges on ASML and TSMC earnings later this week.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically oversold — earnings binary approaches
semiconductors.key_development: Oil shock and CPI fears drove a -5.2% SOXX rout. ASML (tomorrow) and TSMC (Thursday) earnings are the next catalysts.

big_tech.summary: BigTech held up relatively better, with AMZN and MSFT flat to slightly positive. GOOG slipped further below its 50-day SMA but carries a 3.2:1 R:R ahead of earnings in 8 days — potential post-print opportunity. PLTR’s government thesis is strong but valuation remains extreme. The cluster is in wait-and-see mode: more insulated from the oil shock but not offering clean entries.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk remains
big_tech.key_development: MSFT announced a $7B AI data center with Chevron, reinforcing the infrastructure narrative. OpenAI’s ad business miss is a minor overhang for MSFT-OpenAI valuations.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA’s other $2B photonics partner; AI optical interconnect buildout directly links to LITE and the broader semiconductor photonics trend.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10-12% from current levels and 800G transceiver demand holds above expectations.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Photonics deal with Tower Semi (5M PICs shipped) and custom ASIC exposure — links directly to AVGO’s AI ASIC thesis and optical networking demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL’s upcoming earnings confirm >$200M quarterly photonics revenue and the stock reclaims its 50-day SMA.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI networking leader for hyperscale cloud — Vera Rubin scaling and hyperscaler capex directly lift Arista’s switch demand, a read-through to NVDA and CRWV.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET reports Q2 results showing >35% YoY growth in AI-driven switching and the stock holds above its 200-day SMA.

active_risks: US reinstates blockade of Iranian ships in Strait of Hormuz and proposes 20% transit fee, escalating military tensions per Bloomberg.
China bans helium exports, threatening semiconductor manufacturing gas supply amid renewed US-Iran conflict, per China MOFCOM.
Ukraine targets Russian tanker in Sea of Azov, intensifying logistics lockdown, per Crypto Briefing.
Fed rate-hike odds rise to 46.5% (CME) as oil surge rekindles inflation fears ahead of June CPI release.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz escalation sent WTI crashing 7.1% higher, punishing rate-sensitive semis (SOXX -5.2%, NVDA -3.4%). SK Hynix and Samsung both slumped over 5% in Korea, while BigTech held up better. Oil-driven inflation concerns directly threaten the Fed’s pause narrative, keeping capital on the sidelines until today’s CPI.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced reinstatement of Hormuz blockade and a 20% transit fee; US airstrikes targeted Iranian energy infrastructure. Oil surged 7.1%, the biggest one-day jump since April.

base.description: Strait of Hormuz tensions persist but do not escalate to a full-scale war; WTI stays near $80-85. Semis remain under pressure but find support after ASML/TSMC earnings. Fed holds steady.

optimistic.description: Ceasefire or de-escalation leads to WTI dropping back to $70-75, cooling inflation fears. CPI comes in softer, easing rate-hike odds. Semis and BigTech rally sharply, with NVDA reclaiming its 50-day SMA.

pessimistic.description: US-Iran conflict escalates into direct military confrontation, sending WTI above $100. Semis sell off further (SOXX down another 5-10%) as inflation fears force the Fed to signal a July hike. High-beta names (NVTS, CRWV) crash.

wildcard.description: China offers to mediate the US-Iran conflict and ties the helium export ban to progress in trade talks, creating a sudden de-escalation in technology supply chains. Semis sharply reverse on dual-news relief.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold existing positions, no new entries until VIX subsides and CPI clears; semis on watch for oversold bounce after ASML/TSMC earnings.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — risk-off with oil shock, awaiting CPI
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 3 of 31 names are hard-blocked on technical extension (Singapore banks), but the broader watchlist is grappling with a US-Iran escalation spike and heavy semis losses; the June CPI report this morning is the make-or-break event.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: CPI cools to 4.0% or below, reducing rate-hike odds and reviving risk appetite.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI re-accelerates above 4.5%, cementing a July rate hike and extending semis selloff.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Strong Q2 beat, raised full-year outlook and High-NA EUV order acceleration — thesis solidifies.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Q2 miss, guidance cut or DUV order slowdown signals softening AI demand — semis crack.
bearish_impact: TSM → CAUTION on fear of foundry slowdown

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Blowout quarter with 68% monthly sales jump sustained, AI demand guide solid — validates supply chain.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Guidance disappoints or capex reduced, raising doubts on AI spend sustainability.

first_pass_critical_violations: CBRS: signal=CAUTION writeup.what_to_do lacks a numeric re-entry zone. Per FIELD 3 and the depth mandate, quote the pre-computed anchor verbatim: reentry_zone.level = '$196.73' (support at $196.73 (4% below)). Keep your own date/event trigger alongside it if you have one.

NVTS: headline says 'Promoted to AVOID' but signal='AVOID' prev_signal='AVOID'. Per SIGNAL CHANGE TRANSPARENCY (PRIORITY 2), 'Promoted to AVOID' requires today's signal=AVOID and prev_signal != AVOID. Use 'Maintained AVOID' or another framing.

contradictions.sentence: Oil-driven inflation fears rekindled a 46.5% probability of a July Fed rate hike, according to CME FedWatch, ahead of today's critical CPI report.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=6) / ACCUMULATE (n=9) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
